basics.name: Kwok Yuen FAN
basics.label: Assistant Professor of Economics and Finance
basics.summary: Assistant Professor of Economics and Finance at the Hang Seng University of Hong Kong with a Ph.D. in Real Estate Economics. Research focuses on Sustainability, Real Estate Economics, and Corporate Finance. Published in reputable journals including World Development and the International Review of Financial Analysis. Passed CFA Level III and FRM Part II examinations.

work.name: The Hang Seng University of Hong Kong (HSUHK)
work.position: Assistant Professor of Economics and Finance
work.summary: Teaching and research in the fields of economics and finance.
highlights: Courses Taught: Urban and Real Estate Economics, Business Economics

work.name: Beijing Normal-Hong Kong Baptist University (BNU-HKBU) United International College
work.summary: Achieved high student evaluation scores across multiple finance courses.
highlights: Taught Courses: Financial Forecasting (4.97/5.00), Financial Management (4.96/5.00), Multinational Finance (4.93/5.00), Business Research Method (4.89/5.00), Corporate Finance (4.76/5.00)

work.name: The Hong Kong Polytechnic University
work.summary: Provided teaching assistance and academic support for undergraduate and postgraduate courses.
highlights: Tutorials Led: Development Finance and Investment, Construction Economics, Business Valuation and Accounts

work.name: Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu
work.summary: Gained industry experience in financial audit and assurance services.

courses: Thesis: Institutional designs and environmental governance

education.institution: Nanyang Technological University, Nanyang Business School

courses: Dissertation: The cross-sectional profitability of idiosyncratic volatility and its common pattern for stock returns: Empirical evidence from the U.S. markets

education.institution: The Hang Seng University of Hong Kong

publications.name: Performance measurability, local government’s incentives, and regional air pollution reduction
publications.summary: Finds that enhanced environmental performance measurability improves environmental quality, especially when local leaders have strong career incentives and financial support is tied to environmental outcomes.

publications.name: Why high-speed rail causes heterogeneous spatial patterns in firm innovation: Perspectives from intensive and extensive margins
publications.summary: Decomposes the impact of high-speed rail on innovation, showing it boosts overall innovation through knowledge spillovers but can worsen regional inequality via a 'brain drain' effect.

publications.name: ESG components and equity returns: Evidence from real estate investment trusts
publications.summary: Reveals that for US REITs, environmental performance negatively impacts returns, while social and governance performance are positively associated, highlighting component-specific ESG effects.

publications.name: The value of sustainable property management in real estate: Evidence from Hong Kong
publications.summary: Shows that the ESG performance of property management firms is capitalized into housing prices, driven by reduced perceived risk and higher rental growth prospects.

publications.name: The valuation effect and consequences of clawback adoption in real estate investment trusts
publications.publisher: The Journal of Real Estate Finance and Economics
publications.summary: Demonstrates that investors positively value the adoption of clawback provisions in REITs, leading to improved governance, reduced financial restatements, and enhanced disclosure quality.

publications.name: Did real estate professionals anticipate the 2007-2008 financial crisis? Evidence from insider trading in the REITs
publications.publisher: The Journal of Real Estate Finance and Economics
publications.summary: Finds that REIT insiders anticipated the 2007-2008 crisis, selling holdings as early as 2004, suggesting their trading patterns can act as an early warning for market bubbles.

publications.name: The beta anomaly in the REIT market
publications.publisher: The Journal of Real Estate Finance and Economics
publications.summary: Argues the beta anomaly in REITs is driven by institutional preference for high-beta assets, which inflates their prices and leads to subsequent underperformance.

projects.name: An Institutional Perspective of Selective ESG Engagement
projects.description: Examines how institutional factors (environmental policy, investor attitudes) shape selective ESG engagement in REITs across the U.S., Europe, and Asia, finding that such strategies enhance profitability in the U.S. but reduce it in Europe.
highlights: Status: Awarded Best Conference Paper Prize at the 2024 China Real Estate Academic Annual Conference.

projects.name: Environmental Materiality in ESG Strategy: A REIT Perspective
projects.description: Investigates how REITs strategically prioritize social and governance pillars over the costly environmental pillar, finding this strategy boosts financial performance but leads to adverse environmental outcomes like increased emissions.

projects.name: Lobbying and Fossil Fuel Investment
projects.description: Finds that lobbying by U.S. oil and gas companies is associated with a 6.2% increase in capital expenditures and a 16.4% increase in reserves growth, suggesting lobbying directly encourages fossil fuel investment.

projects.name: Regulatory Arbitrage by Governments and Land Conveyance
projects.description: Shows that following stricter air pollution regulations in some Chinese regions, bordering counties strategically reduced land prices to attract polluting industries, leading to localized economic growth but worsening pollution.

projects.name: Environmental Regulatory Independence and Corporate Environmental Performance
projects.description: Examines China's 2016 environmental verticalization reform, finding that increased regulator independence significantly improved the environmental performance of local state-owned enterprises without negatively affecting their financial performance.

projects.name: Climate Lobbying under uncertainty: Corporate Strategies and consequences
projects.description: Analyzes corporate strategies and consequences related to climate lobbying in a context of policy uncertainty.

projects.name: Leverage constraints and financial manipulation in the real estate sector
projects.description: A study on the interplay between leverage constraints and financial manipulation practices in the real estate industry.

projects.name: Can fintech enhance household climate resilience? Evidence from P2P lending platform
projects.description: Investigates the potential for P2P lending platforms to improve households' ability to withstand climate-related financial shocks.

awards.title: Best Conference Paper Prize
awards.awarder: China Real Estate Academic Annual Conference
awards.summary: Paper: An Institutional Perspective of Selective ESG Engagement

awards.title: Best Conference Paper Prize
awards.awarder: China Real Estate Academic Annual Conference
awards.summary: Paper: The Value of Sustainable Property Management in Real Estate: Evidence from Hong Kong
